pubmed.title: Pancreatic Cancer: Changing Epidemiology and New Approaches to Risk Assessment, Early Detection, and Prevention.
Advances in the epidemiology of pancreatic cancer: Trends, risk factors, screening, and prognosis.

open_targets.name: pancreatic neoplasm
open_targets.description: Neoplasm located in the pancreas. Depending on the types of ISLET CELLS present in the tumors, various hormones can be secreted: GLUCAGON from PANCREATIC ALPHA CELLS; INSULIN from PANCREATIC BETA CELLS; and SOMATOSTATIN from the SOMATOSTATIN-SECRETING CELLS. Most are malignant except the insulin-producing tumors (INSULINOMA).

open_targets.name: pancreatic carcinoma
open_targets.description: A carcinoma that arises from epithelial cells of the exocrine pancreas

open_targets.name: Malignant Pancreatic Neoplasm
open_targets.description: A malignant neoplasm involving the pancreas.

open_targets.name: Neoplasm of the pancreas
open_targets.description: A tumor (abnormal growth of tissue) of the pancreas.

open_targets.name: pancreatic cancer, susceptibility to, 4
open_targets.description: Any familial pancreatic carcinoma in which the cause of the disease is a mutation in the BRCA1 gene.

hypotheses.title: Metformin as an OXPHOS‑targeted metabolic stressor in KRAS‑mutant pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma
hypotheses.mechanism: Metformin inhibits complex I of the mitochondrial electron transport chain, reducing oxidative phosphorylation and ATP production, leading to energetic crisis and apoptosis selectively in KRAS‑driven PDAC cells that rely on oxidative phosphorylation.
hypotheses.rationale: While KRAS activation predominantly drives glycolytic flux, a subset of PDAC exhibits high basal OXPHOS activity; metformin has demonstrated antineoplastic activity in metabolic‑vulnerable tumors and is under‑explored in PDAC; preclinical data suggest synthetic lethality with KRAS signaling.
hypotheses.next_test: Phase II neoadjuvant trial of metformin (500 mg BID) combined with standard chemoradiation in resectable KRAS‑mutant PDAC.

hypotheses.title: Circulating tumor DNA methylation signatures for early detection in high‑risk familial pancreatic cancer
hypotheses.mechanism: Hypermethylation of promoter regions such as CADM1 and miR‑196a2 in ctDNA reflects pancreatic epithelial injury and can be quantified in plasma.
hypotheses.rationale: Epidemiologic studies indicate rising incidence in families with inherited risk; current imaging lacks sensitivity for early stages; methylation markers reported in pathogenesis literature provide a non‑invasive biomarker avenue.
hypotheses.next_test: Retrospective validation of a methylation panel (CADM1, miR‑196a2, EPB41L3) in plasma from participants with BRCA1/2 mutations.

hypotheses.title: TGF‑β blockade to remodel the desmoplastic stromal reaction and improve drug delivery
hypotheses.mechanism: Neutralizing antibodies against TGF‑β1 reduce pancreatic stellate cell activation and extracellular matrix deposition, decreasing interstitial pressure.
hypotheses.rationale: Pathogenesis studies describe the central role of stromal fibrosis in PDAC progression and chemoresistance; targeting the stromal niche is an underexplored therapeutic lever.
hypotheses.next_test: Phase I dose‑escalation of a TGF‑β1 neutralizing antibody in combination with gemcitabine in locally advanced PDAC.

hypotheses.title: Omega‑3 fatty acid supplementation to attenuate NF‑κB–driven inflammatory microenvironments
hypotheses.mechanism: EPA/DHA metabolites resolve inflammation by shifting macrophage polarization from M2 to M1 and inhibiting NF‑κB transcriptional activity.
hypotheses.rationale: Epidemiological analyses link high red meat and low fish intake with increased PDAC risk; omega‑3 intake is inversely correlated with inflammation markers; modulating tumor‑associated immunity offers a host‑dietary intervention.
hypotheses.next_test: Randomized controlled pilot of 2 g EPA/DHA daily adjunct to standard chemotherapy in newly diagnosed PDAC patients.

hypotheses.title: PYK2 inhibition to uncover synthetic lethality with EGFR blockade in KRAS‑mutant PDAC
hypotheses.mechanism: PYK2, a KRAS‑interacting kinase, sustains MAPK reactivation; its inhibition abolishes compensatory signaling, rendering cells dependent on EGFR.
hypotheses.rationale: Structural and functional studies of KRAS signaling identify neomorphic activities; PYK2’s role is understudied, representing a novel drug target.
hypotheses.next_test: Preclinical screening of PYK2 inhibitors (e.g., PF‑06650833) in KRAS G12C‑mutant PDAC organoids followed by EGFR inhibitor combination studies.

hypotheses.title: Targeting extracellular mutant KRAS G12D peptides presented on tumor exosomes for immune‑based therapy
hypotheses.mechanism: Exosome‑associated mutant KRAS peptides can be captured by dendritic cells; vaccine design uses Hsp70‑based platforms to elicit cytotoxic T‑cell responses.
hypotheses.rationale: Pathogenesis research shows tumor‑derived exosomes carry oncogenic peptides; leveraging this immune presentation is a novel strategy not yet pursued in PDAC.
hypotheses.next_test: Phase I trial of an Hsp70‑KRAS G12D peptide vaccine in patients with metastatic KRAS G12D‑positive PDAC.

hypotheses.title: Gut microbiota‑derived short‑chain fatty acids to upregulate tumor suppressor miR‑34a
hypotheses.mechanism: Butyrate and propionate inhibit histone deacetylases, enhancing transcription of miR‑34a, which downregulates Notch and BCL‑2, counteracting KRAS‑driven oncogenesis.
hypotheses.rationale: Epidemiological shifts in diet and microbiome composition influence PDAC risk; restoring beneficial SCFA production offers a host‑modulatory approach.
hypotheses.next_test: Preclinical colonized germ‑free mice with human microbiota and evaluate miR‑34a levels and tumor growth after butyrate supplementation.

hypotheses.title: Glucose transporter GLUT2 inhibition to deprive PDAC of systemic hyperglycemia‑driven fuel
hypotheses.mechanism: Blocking GLUT2 reduces hepatic glucose uptake and systemic glucose availability, limiting the glycemic surge that fuels KRAS‑driven tumor metabolism.
hypotheses.rationale: Metabolic reprogramming analyses highlight dependence of PDAC on elevated blood glucose; targeting GLUT2 is a host‑level intervention distinct from tumor‑cell intrinsic targets.
hypotheses.next_test: Pharmacokinetic study of a GLUT2 inhibitor (e.g., GlcNAc analog) in murine PDAC models with high circulating glucose.

hypotheses.title: Repurposing BRAF inhibitors for rare BRAF V600E pancreatic lesions in familial susceptibility cohorts
hypotheses.mechanism: BRAF V600E mutations, though rare, are enriched in families with BRCA1/2 predisposition; MAPK inhibition leads to tumor regression.
hypotheses.rationale: Genetic studies of familial pancreatic cancer susceptibility (MONDO_0013685) identify occasional BRAF alterations; targeting them with existing inhibitors provides a precision avenue.
hypotheses.next_test: Molecular screening of BRAF V600E in BRCA1/2 mutation carriers with pancreatic cancer and prospective treatment with vemurafenib.

hypotheses.title: Immunotherapy against aberrant sialylated glycans on pancreatic tumor cells
hypotheses.mechanism: Tumor cells display excessive α2,6‑linked sialic acid residues; monoclonal antibodies or CAR‑T cells directed at sialyl‑Lewis^a can trigger ADCC and tumor clearance.
hypotheses.rationale: Disease pathology descriptions emphasize dysregulated glycosylation in pancreatic neoplasia; targeting these glycans offers a novel immune checkpoint–like strategy.
hypotheses.next_test: Binding affinity assays of anti‑sialyl‑Lewis^a antibodies in patient‑derived PDAC cell lines followed by in‑vivo tumor growth inhibition.
